invalid_review_attempts.disposition: Rejected by hardening normalizer; not counted as valid pack-level Claude review evidence.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-179 (RP05 Matter Core, risk B, 40 units, RP05.P02.M07.S07-M09.S02) is honestly mid-construction and its pack-level production_ready=false claim is accurate and supported across every artifact (command-evidence, construction-inspection, adjudication, contract). No over-claim was found. (a) Production-ready support: implementation (service.js, registry.js, validators.js, model.js), tests (model.test.js), and contract are internally consistent with production_ready=false; production_ready_candidate is correctly gated on coverage.valid && serviceEvidence.valid. (b) No-write / no-real-data boundary holds: freezeServiceResult() stamps writes_product_state=false, creates_database_rows=false, evaluates_runtime_permission=false, writes_audit_event=false, acquires_runtime_lock=false on all five workflows; fixtures are synthetic_only=true / uses_real_client_data=false; product runtime imports no fs/net/db (node:fs appears only in test-only config reads). (c) Authority boundary correct: registry CP179 attestation sets promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false; contract service_evidence_review_bridge sets claude_is_final_approval=false; Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer, not final approver. (d) Hermes is confined to deterministic control-plane review-packet/receipt/evidence generators and gate IDs (H05/C05) plus the baseline generator which writes only into the review-artifact directory; no Hermes runtime is embedded in the product or apps/. CP178+ review hardening is working as designed: the prior pack-level review attempt failed with status 143 / 0-byte stdout and was correctly rejected as invalid (auth preflight had passed, so this was not an auth failure); a valid normalized review receipt is still required before closeout, which this re-run supplies. All open items are downstream steps expected to be pending at review step 3 of 8 and do not block deliverable soundness. No P0 or P1 defects; three P3 advisories only.

findings.title: Prior invalid review attempt not recorded in result ledger's invalid_review_attempts
findings.details: claude-review-result.json has status review_pending with exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run=false, valid_review_receipts=[] and invalid_review_attempts=[], yet review_receipt_ref points to an invalid receipt (status invalid_review, valid_review=false, invalid_reason 'stdout_0_bytes; required_field_error: parsed_review missing', review_execution.status=143). The prior 0-byte/SIGTERM attempt was correctly rejected by the hardening but was not logged into the invalid_review_attempts array, so the result ledger under-counts attempts. This is a bookkeeping gap, not a soundness or boundary defect; the hardening still blocks closeout because no valid receipt exists yet.
findings.recommendation: During final closeout, record this run as the one valid normalized receipt in valid_review_receipts, set exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run=true, and backfill the rejected status-143 attempt into invalid_review_attempts so the ledger reflects the full attempt history.

findings.title: Adjudication, construction-inspection, and command-evidence are pending with empty validation command sets
findings.details: command-evidence.json has commands=[] with production_ready=false and the H05 gate pending; construction-inspection.json verdict=PENDING with tests_and_validation.commands=[], all_passed=false, h05_gate_passed=false, c05_gate_passed=false; adjudication.md states the pack is scaffolded only. This is the expected state because these are steps 6-8 of the hardened sequence and run downstream of the Claude review (step 3). It is flagged only so the gap is explicitly tracked, not because it indicates an over-claim.
findings.recommendation: At final closeout, execute the 11 expected validation commands, populate the executed-command evidence, and flip h05_gate_passed/c05_gate_passed (and production_ready) only after they pass with claude_not_final_approver and enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation preserved.

findings.title: review_status is validated against lifecycle_statuses for all model types
findings.details: validateMatterCoreRecord validates record.review_status membership against definition.lifecycle_statuses for every model type, coupling the review_status vocabulary to lifecycle_statuses generically rather than per-type. There is no runtime or no-write boundary impact and the existing tests pass; this is a minor vocabulary-coupling observation only.
findings.recommendation: If review_status and lifecycle_status vocabularies are intended to diverge per model type in a later pack, separate the allowed-value sets; otherwise document that the coupling is intentional. No action required for CP00-179 closeout.
